title: Nassella cernua
taxonomy: Nassella cernua is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Nassella, which is a group of grasses native to the Americas. Nassella cernua is further classified as a member of the tribe Stipeae. Within this tribe, it is placed in the subtribe Nassellinae. Nassella cernua is considered a synonym of the species Nassella lepida.

intro: Nassella cernua, also known as Purple Needlegrass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Nassella cernua include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Nassella cernua is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in tinctures and extracts, to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
